description: Norteños are affiliated gangs paying tribute to Nuestra Familia in California prisons, primarily composed of Mexican Americans. Founded in 1968 as a prison rival to Sureños, they operate mainly in Northern California and other states. They use the number 14 and color red to signify affiliation. Their criminal activities include drug trafficking, murder, and robbery.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Mostly in Northern California , Arizona , New Mexico , Washington (state) , Oregon , Nevada , Utah , Idaho , Montana , Wyoming , South Dakota , Colorado , Kansas , Arkansas , Maryland , Virginia , North Carolina , South Carolina , Oklahoma , Illinois , Indiana , Florida , Mexico , Texas
country_links.context: The Norteños have territory in Mexico.
